content.text:
{"success":true,"process_rule_id":"J09A","process_rule":"1. Create working copy table.
   1.1. From table `decision_table`, select all rows where column `TOURS` starts with the prefix `"J09A"` (string starts-with comparison).
   1.2. Copy those rows, with all their columns, into a new table named `J09A_temp`.

2. Add decision column.
   2.1. Add a new column named `ENTSCHEIDUNG` to table `J09A_temp`.
   2.2. Use a text / string type that can contain the values `"KSA"` and `"EVU"`.
   2.3. Initialize all values in `ENTSCHEIDUNG` to empty / NULL.

3. Set `ENTSCHEIDUNG` values in `J09A_temp` according to rules, in the specified order.

   3.1. Rule 1: Based on number of meters (EQUNR) per house (HAUS).
        3.1.1. For each distinct value of `HAUS` in `J09A_temp`, count the number of distinct `EQUNR` values associated with that `HAUS`. Call this `EQUNR_count_for_HAUS`.
        3.1.2. For each row in `J09A_temp`:
               If `EQUNR_count_for_HAUS` < 3:
                   3.1.2.1. Set `ENTSCHEIDUNG` for that row to `"KSA"`.
               Else:
                   3.1.2.2. Set `ENTSCHEIDUNG` for that row to `"EVU"`.

   3.2. Rule 2: Override for `HOUSE_NUM1 = 0`.
        3.2.1. For each row in `J09A_temp`:
               If `HOUSE_NUM1` = 0:
                   3.2.1.1. Set `ENTSCHEIDUNG` for that row to `"EVU"` (overwrite any previous value).
               Else:
                   3.2.1.2. Do not change `ENTSCHEIDUNG` for that row.

   3.3. Rule 3: Override for `HOUSE_NUM1` between 500 and 599 (inclusive).
        3.3.1. For each row in `J09A_temp`:
               If `HOUSE_NUM1` >= 500 AND `HOUSE_NUM1` <= 599:
                   3.3.1.1. Set `ENTSCHEIDUNG` for that row to `"EVU"` (overwrite any previous value).
               Else:
                   3.3.1.2. Do not change `ENTSCHEIDUNG` for that row.

4. Join with `EABLG` table on installation.

   4.1. Perform an INNER JOIN between `J09A_temp` and `EABLG`:
        4.1.1. Join condition: `J09A_temp.ANLAGE` = `EABLG.INSTALLATION` (or the equivalent installation-ID column in `EABLG`).
        4.1.2. Create a new working table (or view) from this join; call it `J09A_join_EABLG`.
        4.1.3. Include all columns from `J09A_temp` plus all needed columns from `EABLG`, including `Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.`.

5. Join with `NET_MASTER` and `SALES_MASTER` on `EQUNR`.

   5.1. Join with `NET_MASTER`.
        5.1.1. Perform an INNER JOIN between `J09A_join_EABLG` and `NET_MASTER`.
        5.1.2. Join condition: `J09A_join_EABLG.EQUNR` = `NET_MASTER.EQUNR`.
        5.1.3. Create intermediate table `J09A_net` containing:
               5.1.3.1. All columns from `J09A_join_EABLG`.
               5.1.3.2. All relevant columns from `NET_MASTER` (e.g., identification, address, and contact fields).
        5.1.4. If column names from `NET_MASTER` duplicate existing column names:
               5.1.4.1. Either use table aliases or rename the `NET_MASTER` columns with a `NET_` prefix to distinguish them.

   5.2. Join with `SALES_MASTER`.
        5.2.1. Perform an INNER JOIN between `J09A_net` and `SALES_MASTER`.
        5.2.2. Join condition: `J09A_net.EQUNR` = `SALES_MASTER.EQUNR`.
        5.2.3. Create table `J09A_full` containing:
               5.2.3.1. All columns from `J09A_net`.
               5.2.3.2. All relevant columns from `SALES_MASTER` (e.g., identification, address, and contact fields).
        5.2.4. If column names from `SALES_MASTER` duplicate existing column names:
               5.2.4.1. Either use table aliases or rename the `SALES_MASTER` columns with a `SALES_` prefix to distinguish them.

6. Decide per row whether to use SALES data or NET data.

   6.1. For each row in `J09A_full`, determine whether corresponding SALES data exists.
        6.1.1. Treat SALES data as “present” if at least one identifying column from `SALES_MASTER` (e.g., `SALES_MASTER.ME_MA_ID` or `SALES_MASTER.EQUNR`) is non-NULL after the join.
        6.1.2. Otherwise, treat SALES data as “not present”.

   6.2. For each row in `J09A_full`:
        If SALES data is present for that row:
            6.2.1. Use SALES data as the source for the relevant output fields:
                   6.2.1.1. Set `ME_MA_ID` to the value from `SALES_MASTER`.
                   6.2.1.2. Set `TITLE`, `FORENAME`, `SURNAME`, `STREET`, `STREETNO`, `POST_CODE1`, `CITY1`, `CITY2`, `EMAIL` to the corresponding values from `SALES_MASTER`.
            6.2.2. Set all NET-based equivalents used only as an alternative source (e.g., NET_MASTER’s versions of the above fields) to empty / NULL for this row.
        Else:
            6.2.3. Use NET data as the source for the relevant output fields:
                   6.2.3.1. Set `ME_MA_ID` to the value from `NET_MASTER` (or the appropriate NET identifier).
                   6.2.3.2. Set `TITLE`, `FORENAME`, `SURNAME`, `STREET`, `STREETNO`, `POST_CODE1`, `CITY1`, `CITY2`, `EMAIL` to the corresponding values from `NET_MASTER`.
            6.2.4. Set all SALES-based equivalents used only as an alternative source to empty / NULL for this row.

7. Export final CSV `J09A.csv`.

   7.1. From `J09A_full` (after applying step 6), select exactly the following columns for export:
        7.1.1. `Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.`
        7.1.2. `HAUS`
        7.1.3. `ANLAGE`
        7.1.4. `ME_MA_ID`
        7.1.5. `EQUNR`
        7.1.6. `HOUSE_NUM1`
        7.1.7. `ENTSCHEIDUNG`
        7.1.8. `TITLE`
        7.1.9. `FORENAME`
        7.1.10. `SURNAME`
        7.1.11. `STREET`
        7.1.12. `STREETNO`
        7.1.13. `POST_CODE1`
        7.1.14. `CITY1`
        7.1.15. `CITY2`
        7.1.16. `EMAIL`
   7.2. Export these selected columns to a CSV file.
   7.3. Name the CSV file `J09A.csv`.

8. Email the CSV to the EVU team.

   8.1. Create an email addressed to `meter.readings@evu.com`.
   8.2. Set a suitable subject line (e.g., `"J09A Meter Reading Data"`).
   8.3. Attach the file `J09A.csv` to the email.
   8.4. Send the email.

9. Clean up temporary data.

   9.1. After confirming that the CSV has been successfully exported and the email sent:
        9.1.1. Delete (DROP) the table `J09A_temp`.
        9.1.2. Optionally also drop any intermediate tables created in this process (e.g., `J09A_join_EABLG`, `J09A_net`, `J09A_full`) if they are not needed further."}

structured_content.process_rule:
1. Create working copy table.
   1.1. From table `decision_table`, select all rows where column `TOURS` starts with the prefix `"J09A"` (string starts-with comparison).
   1.2. Copy those rows, with all their columns, into a new table named `J09A_temp`.

2. Add decision column.
   2.1. Add a new column named `ENTSCHEIDUNG` to table `J09A_temp`.
   2.2. Use a text / string type that can contain the values `"KSA"` and `"EVU"`.
   2.3. Initialize all values in `ENTSCHEIDUNG` to empty / NULL.

3. Set `ENTSCHEIDUNG` values in `J09A_temp` according to rules, in the specified order.

   3.1. Rule 1: Based on number of meters (EQUNR) per house (HAUS).
        3.1.1. For each distinct value of `HAUS` in `J09A_temp`, count the number of distinct `EQUNR` values associated with that `HAUS`. Call this `EQUNR_count_for_HAUS`.
        3.1.2. For each row in `J09A_temp`:
               If `EQUNR_count_for_HAUS` < 3:
                   3.1.2.1. Set `ENTSCHEIDUNG` for that row to `"KSA"`.
               Else:
                   3.1.2.2. Set `ENTSCHEIDUNG` for that row to `"EVU"`.

   3.2. Rule 2: Override for `HOUSE_NUM1 = 0`.
        3.2.1. For each row in `J09A_temp`:
               If `HOUSE_NUM1` = 0:
                   3.2.1.1. Set `ENTSCHEIDUNG` for that row to `"EVU"` (overwrite any previous value).
               Else:
                   3.2.1.2. Do not change `ENTSCHEIDUNG` for that row.

   3.3. Rule 3: Override for `HOUSE_NUM1` between 500 and 599 (inclusive).
        3.3.1. For each row in `J09A_temp`:
               If `HOUSE_NUM1` >= 500 AND `HOUSE_NUM1` <= 599:
                   3.3.1.1. Set `ENTSCHEIDUNG` for that row to `"EVU"` (overwrite any previous value).
               Else:
                   3.3.1.2. Do not change `ENTSCHEIDUNG` for that row.

4. Join with `EABLG` table on installation.

   4.1. Perform an INNER JOIN between `J09A_temp` and `EABLG`:
        4.1.1. Join condition: `J09A_temp.ANLAGE` = `EABLG.INSTALLATION` (or the equivalent installation-ID column in `EABLG`).
        4.1.2. Create a new working table (or view) from this join; call it `J09A_join_EABLG`.
        4.1.3. Include all columns from `J09A_temp` plus all needed columns from `EABLG`, including `Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.`.

5. Join with `NET_MASTER` and `SALES_MASTER` on `EQUNR`.

   5.1. Join with `NET_MASTER`.
        5.1.1. Perform an INNER JOIN between `J09A_join_EABLG` and `NET_MASTER`.
        5.1.2. Join condition: `J09A_join_EABLG.EQUNR` = `NET_MASTER.EQUNR`.
        5.1.3. Create intermediate table `J09A_net` containing:
               5.1.3.1. All columns from `J09A_join_EABLG`.
               5.1.3.2. All relevant columns from `NET_MASTER` (e.g., identification, address, and contact fields).
        5.1.4. If column names from `NET_MASTER` duplicate existing column names:
               5.1.4.1. Either use table aliases or rename the `NET_MASTER` columns with a `NET_` prefix to distinguish them.

   5.2. Join with `SALES_MASTER`.
        5.2.1. Perform an INNER JOIN between `J09A_net` and `SALES_MASTER`.
        5.2.2. Join condition: `J09A_net.EQUNR` = `SALES_MASTER.EQUNR`.
        5.2.3. Create table `J09A_full` containing:
               5.2.3.1. All columns from `J09A_net`.
               5.2.3.2. All relevant columns from `SALES_MASTER` (e.g., identification, address, and contact fields).
        5.2.4. If column names from `SALES_MASTER` duplicate existing column names:
               5.2.4.1. Either use table aliases or rename the `SALES_MASTER` columns with a `SALES_` prefix to distinguish them.

6. Decide per row whether to use SALES data or NET data.

   6.1. For each row in `J09A_full`, determine whether corresponding SALES data exists.
        6.1.1. Treat SALES data as “present” if at least one identifying column from `SALES_MASTER` (e.g., `SALES_MASTER.ME_MA_ID` or `SALES_MASTER.EQUNR`) is non-NULL after the join.
        6.1.2. Otherwise, treat SALES data as “not present”.

   6.2. For each row in `J09A_full`:
        If SALES data is present for that row:
            6.2.1. Use SALES data as the source for the relevant output fields:
                   6.2.1.1. Set `ME_MA_ID` to the value from `SALES_MASTER`.
                   6.2.1.2. Set `TITLE`, `FORENAME`, `SURNAME`, `STREET`, `STREETNO`, `POST_CODE1`, `CITY1`, `CITY2`, `EMAIL` to the corresponding values from `SALES_MASTER`.
            6.2.2. Set all NET-based equivalents used only as an alternative source (e.g., NET_MASTER’s versions of the above fields) to empty / NULL for this row.
        Else:
            6.2.3. Use NET data as the source for the relevant output fields:
                   6.2.3.1. Set `ME_MA_ID` to the value from `NET_MASTER` (or the appropriate NET identifier).
                   6.2.3.2. Set `TITLE`, `FORENAME`, `SURNAME`, `STREET`, `STREETNO`, `POST_CODE1`, `CITY1`, `CITY2`, `EMAIL` to the corresponding values from `NET_MASTER`.
            6.2.4. Set all SALES-based equivalents used only as an alternative source to empty / NULL for this row.

7. Export final CSV `J09A.csv`.

   7.1. From `J09A_full` (after applying step 6), select exactly the following columns for export:
        7.1.1. `Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.`
        7.1.2. `HAUS`
        7.1.3. `ANLAGE`
        7.1.4. `ME_MA_ID`
        7.1.5. `EQUNR`
        7.1.6. `HOUSE_NUM1`
        7.1.7. `ENTSCHEIDUNG`
        7.1.8. `TITLE`
        7.1.9. `FORENAME`
        7.1.10. `SURNAME`
        7.1.11. `STREET`
        7.1.12. `STREETNO`
        7.1.13. `POST_CODE1`
        7.1.14. `CITY1`
        7.1.15. `CITY2`
        7.1.16. `EMAIL`
   7.2. Export these selected columns to a CSV file.
   7.3. Name the CSV file `J09A.csv`.

8. Email the CSV to the EVU team.

   8.1. Create an email addressed to `meter.readings@evu.com`.
   8.2. Set a suitable subject line (e.g., `"J09A Meter Reading Data"`).
   8.3. Attach the file `J09A.csv` to the email.
   8.4. Send the email.

9. Clean up temporary data.

   9.1. After confirming that the CSV has been successfully exported and the email sent:
        9.1.1. Delete (DROP) the table `J09A_temp`.
        9.1.2. Optionally also drop any intermediate tables created in this process (e.g., `J09A_join_EABLG`, `J09A_net`, `J09A_full`) if they are not needed further.

args.sqlite_command: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS J09A_temp;

structured_content.schema:
# Table: net_master
## column_name (type) description
EQUNR (TEXT) Equipment Number
TITLE (TEXT) Title (Mr., Mrs., Dr., etc.)
FORENAME (TEXT) First name
SURNAME (TEXT) Last name/surname
STREET (TEXT) Street name
STREETNO (TEXT) Street number
CITY1 (TEXT) City name
CITY2 (TEXT) District/additional city information
POST_CODE1 (INTEGER) Postal code
EMAIL (TEXT) Email address

# Table: sales_master
## column_name (type) description
EQUNR (TEXT) Equipment Number
TITLE (TEXT) Title (Mr., Mrs., Dr., etc.)
FORENAME (TEXT) First name
SURNAME (TEXT) Last name/surname
STREET (TEXT) Street name
STREETNO (TEXT) Street number
CITY1 (TEXT) City name
CITY2 (TEXT) District/additional city information
POST_CODE1 (INTEGER) Postal code
EMAIL (TEXT) Email address

# Table: decision_table
## column_name (type) description
HAUS (TEXT) House identifier
ANLAGE (TEXT) Installation/facility identifier
ABLEINH (TEXT) Reading unit identifier
TOUR (TEXT) Tour/route identifier
ME_MA_ID (TEXT) Meter/Measuring point ID
CITY1 (TEXT) City name
CITY2 (TEXT) District/additional city information
HOUSE_NUM1 (INTEGER) House/building number
POST_CODE1 (INTEGER) Postal code
EQUNR (TEXT) Equipment Number

# Table: eabl
## column_name (type) description
Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No. (TEXT) Meter Reading Document Number
EQUNR (TEXT) Equipment Number
Geplante Ableseart (INTEGER) Planned reading type
MR type (TEXT) Meter reading type code
MR TYPE TEXT (TEXT) Meter Reading type description
MR category (TEXT) Meter reading category
ISTABLART_TXT (TEXT) Actual reading type description (SAP)
LOGIKZW (TEXT) Logic indicator
KENNZIFF (TEXT) Identification number
Record created on (TEXT) Record creation date
Object changed on (TEXT) Object modification date

# Table: eablg
## column_name (type) description
Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No. (TEXT) Meter Reading Document Number
Installation (TEXT) Installation identifier
Meter Reading reason (INTEGER) Meter reading reason code
MR Reason - Text (TEXT) Meter reading reason description
Scheduled MR Date (TEXT) Scheduled meter reading date
Meter Reading unit (TEXT) Meter reading unit
Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No..1 (INTEGER) Secondary/duplicate Meter Reading Document Number

# Table: eanl
## column_name (type) description
Installation (TEXT) Installation identifier
Installation type (TEXT) Type of installation
Record created on (TEXT) Record creation date
Object changed on (TEXT) Object modification date
SPARTE_TEXT (TEXT) Division/sector description
AKLASSE (TEXT) Customer class code
AKLASSE_TEXT (TEXT) Customer class description
TARIFTYP (TEXT) Tariff type code
TARIFTYP_TEXT (TEXT) Tariff type description
BRANCHE (TEXT) Industry/sector code
BRANCHE_TEXT (TEXT) Industry/sector description
ABLEINH (TEXT) Reading unit identifier
ME_MA_ID (TEXT) Meter/Measuring point ID
HAUS (TEXT) House identifier

# Table: process_rules
## column_name (type) description
process_rule_id (TEXT) PRIMARY KEY - Process rule identifier
process_rule (TEXT) NOT NULL - Process rule text/description

args.sqlite_command: ALTER TABLE J09A_temp ADD COLUMN ENTSCHEIDUNG TEXT;
UPDATE J09A_temp SET ENTSCHEIDUNG = NULL;

WITH haus_counts AS (
  SELECT HAUS, COUNT(DISTINCT EQUNR) AS EQUNR_count_for_HAUS
  FROM J09A_temp
  GROUP BY HAUS
)
UPDATE J09A_temp
SET ENTSCHEIDUNG = CASE
  WHEN haus_counts.EQUNR_count_for_HAUS < 3 THEN 'KSA'
  ELSE 'EVU'
END
FROM haus_counts
WHERE J09A_temp.HAUS = haus_counts.HAUS;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS J09A_join_EABLG;

CREATE TABLE J09A_join_EABLG AS
SELECT
  t.*,
  e."Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.",
  e."Meter Reading reason",
  e."MR Reason - Text",
  e."Scheduled MR Date",
  e."Meter Reading unit",
  e."Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No..1"
FROM J09A_temp t
INNER JOIN eablg e
  ON t.ANLAGE = e.Installation;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS J09A_net;

CREATE TABLE J09A_net AS
SELECT
  j.*,
  n.TITLE AS NET_TITLE,
  n.FORENAME AS NET_FORENAME,
  n.SURNAME AS NET_SURNAME,
  n.STREET AS NET_STREET,
  n.STREETNO AS NET_STREETNO,
  n.POST_CODE1 AS NET_POST_CODE1,
  n.CITY1 AS NET_CITY1,
  n.CITY2 AS NET_CITY2,
  n.EMAIL AS NET_EMAIL
FROM J09A_join_EABLG j
INNER JOIN net_master n
  ON j.EQUNR = n.EQUNR;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS J09A_full;

CREATE TABLE J09A_full AS
SELECT
  jn.*,
  s.TITLE AS SALES_TITLE,
  s.FORENAME AS SALES_FORENAME,
  s.SURNAME AS SALES_SURNAME,
  s.STREET AS SALES_STREET,
  s.STREETNO AS SALES_STREETNO,
  s.POST_CODE1 AS SALES_POST_CODE1,
  s.CITY1 AS SALES_CITY1,
  s.CITY2 AS SALES_CITY2,
  s.EMAIL AS SALES_EMAIL
FROM J09A_net jn
LEFT JOIN sales_master s
  ON jn.EQUNR = s.EQUNR;
ALTER TABLE J09A_full ADD COLUMN TITLE TEXT;
ALTER TABLE J09A_full ADD COLUMN FORENAME TEXT;
ALTER TABLE J09A_full ADD COLUMN SURNAME TEXT;
ALTER TABLE J09A_full ADD COLUMN STREET TEXT;
ALTER TABLE J09A_full ADD COLUMN STREETNO TEXT;
ALTER TABLE J09A_full ADD COLUMN POST_CODE1_OUT INTEGER;
ALTER TABLE J09A_full ADD COLUMN CITY1_OUT TEXT;
ALTER TABLE J09A_full ADD COLUMN CITY2_OUT TEXT;
ALTER TABLE J09A_full ADD COLUMN EMAIL TEXT;
ALTER TABLE J09A_full ADD COLUMN ME_MA_ID_OUT TEXT;

UPDATE J09A_full
SET
  ME_MA_ID_OUT = CASE WHEN SALES_TITLE IS NOT NULL THEN ME_MA_ID ELSE ME_MA_ID END,
  TITLE = COALESCE(SALES_TITLE, NET_TITLE),
  FORENAME = COALESCE(SALES_FORENAME, NET_FORENAME),
  SURNAME = COALESCE(SALES_SURNAME, NET_SURNAME),
  STREET = COALESCE(SALES_STREET, NET_STREET),
  STREETNO = COALESCE(SALES_STREETNO, NET_STREETNO),
  POST_CODE1_OUT = COALESCE(SALES_POST_CODE1, NET_POST_CODE1),
  CITY1_OUT = COALESCE(SALES_CITY1, NET_CITY1),
  CITY2_OUT = COALESCE(SALES_CITY2, NET_CITY2),
  EMAIL = COALESCE(SALES_EMAIL, NET_EMAIL);

args.sql_query:
SELECT
  "Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.",
  HAUS,
  ANLAGE,
  ME_MA_ID_OUT AS ME_MA_ID,
  EQUNR,
  HOUSE_NUM1,
  ENTSCHEIDUNG,
  TITLE,
  FORENAME,
  SURNAME,
  STREET,
  STREETNO,
  POST_CODE1_OUT AS POST_CODE1,
  CITY1_OUT AS CITY1,
  CITY2_OUT AS CITY2,
  EMAIL
FROM J09A_full;

args.email:
Dear EVU Meter Reading Team,

Please find attached the latest J09A meter reading data export.

Best regards,
Automated Process Agent

args.sqlite_command: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS J09A_temp;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS J09A_join_EABLG;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS J09A_net;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS J09A_full;

kwargs.content:
The process for `process_rule_id 'J09A'` has been executed end-to-end:

- Created `J09A_temp` from `decision_table` for TOURs starting with `J09A`.
